title: Olivia Dean - British Singer-Songwriter Biography
text: Olivia Dean was born on March 14, 1999, in the London Borough of Haringey. Her father is English and her mother is of Jamaican and Guyanese descent. Her maternal grandmother, Carmen, emigrated to the United Kingdom from Guyana at age 18 as part of the Windrush generation. Her mother, a barrister, introduced her to the music of Jill Scott, Angie Stone, and Lauryn Hill, while her father introduced her to the music of Carole King and Al Green. By age 8, she knew that she wanted to be a singer, although she was quite shy. She was accepted into a theater course at the BRIT School at age 15 and later switched to studying music. It was at her graduation concert that Dean caught the eye of Emily Braham, who became her manager. In October 2018, Dean self-released her debut single, Reason to Stay. After Reason to Stay was streamed millions of times, she was offered and signed a recording contract with AMF Records and Virgin EMI Records. Her second EP, What Am I Gonna Do On Sundays?, was released in December 2020. In April 2021, she was named the breakthrough artist of the year by Amazon Music. Her debut album, Messy, came out in 2023. Her second studio album, The Art of Loving (2025), catapulted her to global success. In 2026 Dean was awarded the Grammy Award for best new artist.
